summary: Fixed three signal categorization issues in the decomposer: (1) Percentage rounding now uses largest remainder method to always sum to exactly 100.0% — previously naive round() produced 99.9% (76.7+20.9+2.3). (2) Each skill now gets an explicit signal_type field (story_verified|resume_only|inferred) derived from evidence_type via classify_signal_type(). (3) Added 'Inferred' as a valid evidence_type in both skills_assessment and skills_batch LLM prompts with detailed instructions for contextual skill detection (e.g., 'candidate used LangChain/RAG → likely works with embeddings'). Also fixed misclassification where 'Gap' evidence_type was falling into the else branch and being counted as 'inferred' — Gap now correctly maps to 'resume_only'.
testing: Verified with inline unit tests: classify_signal_type correctly maps all evidence_type values including Gap→resume_only and Inferred→inferred. largest_remainder_round produces exact 100.0% sums across all test cases (Hardik data, equal thirds, all-one-type, zero-total). Both decompose.py and chunk_processor.py pass py_compile syntax validation. For Hardik-FlowFuse data with the fix: story_verified=76.7%, resume_only=23.3%, inferred=0.0% (previously 76.7+20.9+2.3=99.9% with Gap misclassified as inferred).

fix_1_rounding.method: Floor all percentages to 1 decimal, distribute deficit to entries with largest fractional remainders

fix_3_inferred_detection.prompt_changes: Added 'Inferred' to evidence_type enum in skills_assessment and skills_batch prompts with examples of contextual inference (Docker from Kubernetes, embeddings from RAG, etc.)
fix_3_inferred_detection.classification_fix: Gap evidence_type no longer falls through to 'inferred' — explicit elif chain handles all cases
